concerns.text: T013 (Pipeline Orchestration) is marked as unchecked [ ] but is a strict producer of the artifact `data/processed/graphs_v1.parquet`. T014 and T015 (Tests) are marked [X] (complete) but depend on the pipeline logic in T013 to exist. A consumer (tests) cannot precede its producer (pipeline). T013 must be moved before T014/T015 or marked complete.

T021 (Inter-family generalization test) depends on T018 (Training) and T017 (Splitting). However, T019a (Success criteria assertion) is marked [X] but logically depends on the output of T021 (the inter-family MAPE) to assert the <15% threshold against unseen families. T019a is currently a consumer of T021's result but appears before it in the list and is marked complete, violating the producer-consumer flow.

T027c (Report generation) depends on T027a (Data aggregation) and T027b (Template generation). T027b is marked [ ] (incomplete) while T027c is marked [ ] (incomplete), but the dependency chain is broken in the 'Within Each User Story' section which lists T027a -> T027b -> T027c. The task list order is correct, but the 'Checkpoint' text claims 'All user stories should now be independently functional' despite T027b and T027c being incomplete, creating a semantic contradiction in the 'independently shippable' claim for US3.

T022a (Disclaimer to JSON) is marked [ ] (incomplete) but is listed after T022 (Integration test) which is marked [X]. The disclaimer is a metadata requirement for the output of T018/T021. If T018 and T021 are considered 'complete' (implied by the checkpoint), T022a should logically precede the finalization of those artifacts or be part of T018/T021. Currently, it sits as a dangling post-processing step that is incomplete, risking the 'independently shippable' status of US2.
